segments.speaker: DAN
segments.en: OK, so now I'm going to show you ...
segments.zh: 好的，现在我要给你看……

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: That seminar yesterday on automation and the future of work was really good, wasn't it?
segments.zh: 昨天那个关于自动化和未来工作的研讨会真的很棒，是吧？回顾19世纪英国第一次工业革命，看看人们如何应对巨大的变化，真是大开眼界。

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: Looking at the first industrial revolution in Britain in the 19th century and seeing how people reacted to massive change was a real eye-opener.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。听到当时人们对自动化的感受以及他们面临的挑战，很有意思。我之前不知道这最初是从纺织业工人开始的。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: It was interesting to hear how people felt about automation then and what challenges they faced.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: I didn't know that first started with workers in the textile industry.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: With those protesting workers called the Luddites destroying their knitting machines because they were so worried about losing their jobs.
segments.zh: 那些抗议的工人被称为卢德分子，他们因为非常担心失去工作而毁坏了织布机。

words.def: 卢德分子（反对技术进步的人）

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: Yes, and ultimately, they didn't achieve anything.
segments.zh: 是的，最终他们什么也没达成。而且，工业化创造的就业机会比它摧毁的要多。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: And anyway, industrialisation created more jobs than it destroyed.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: Yes, that's true – but it probably didn't seem a positive thing at the time.
segments.zh: 是的，没错——但在当时可能看起来并不积极。我能理解为什么卢德分子感到如此受威胁。

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: I can see why the Luddites felt so threatened.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: I know.
segments.zh: 我知道。我肯定也会有同样的感觉。关于未来工作的讨论难得地非常乐观。我喜欢那种工作不再涉及无聊重复任务的想法，因为机器人会做所有那些。通常你只听到关于未来的负面消息。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: I'm sure I would have felt the same.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: The discussion about the future of work was really optimistic for a change.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: I like the idea that work won't involve doing boring, repetitive tasks, as robots will do all that.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: Normally, you only hear negative stuff about the future.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: Bit too optimistic, don't you think?
segments.zh: 有点过于乐观了，你不觉得吗？例如，我看不出人们怎么会有更多的休闲时间，因为所有证据都表明人们花在工作上的时间比以往任何时候都长。

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: For example, I can't see how people are about to have more leisure time, when all the evidence shows people are spending longer than ever at work.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: No – that's true.
segments.zh: 不，那倒是真的。但失业率降低呢？这个我不太确定。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: And what about lower unemployment?

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: I'm not so sure about that.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: Perhaps in the long term – but not in the foreseeable future.
segments.zh: 也许长期来看会，但短期内不太可能。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。而且我猜大多数人会工作到更老的年纪——因为大家的寿命都延长了。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: And I expect most people will be expected to work until they're much older – as everyone's living much longer.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: That's already happening.
segments.zh: 这已经在发生了。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: I enjoyed all that stuff on how technology has changed some jobs and how they're likely to change in the near future.
segments.zh: 我很喜欢那些关于科技如何改变一些工作以及它们未来可能如何变化的内容。

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: Yeah, incredible.
segments.zh: 是啊，太不可思议了。比如会计师。你可能觉得所有技术创新会让他们失业，但实际上会计师比以往任何时候都多。他们仍然很抢手，而且效率高多了。

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: Like accountants.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: You might think all the technological innovations would have put them out of a job, but in fact there are more of them than ever.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: They're still really in demand and have become far more efficient.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 没错。太惊人了。这个国家的会计师数量是19世纪的20倍。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: That was amazing.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: Twenty times more accountants in this country compared to the 19th century.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: I know.
segments.zh: 我知道。我从来没想过理发业的需求在过去一百年里会增长这么多。现在每287个人就有一个理发师，而以前是每1500多人一个。

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: I'd never have thought that demand for hairdressing would have gone up so much in the last hundred years.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: One hairdresser for every 287 people now, compared to one for over 1,500.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: Yeah because people's earning power has gone up so they can afford to spend more on personal services like that.
segments.zh: 是啊，因为人们的赚钱能力提高了，所以他们能花更多钱在像这样的个人服务上。

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: But technology hasn't changed the actual job that much.
segments.zh: 但技术并没有让实际工作改变那么多。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: No, they've got hairdryers, etc. but it's one job where you don't depend on a computer ...
segments.zh: 不，他们有了吹风机之类的，但这是一份不依赖电脑的工作……行政人员的工作因为技术发生了巨大变化。甚至20年前，还有秘书做听写和打字。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: The kind of work that administrative staff do has changed enormously, thanks to technology.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: Even 20 years ago there were secretaries doing dictation and typing.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 是的。和现在相比真的很无聊，现在他们被赋予更多责任和更高地位。

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: Really boring compared to these days, when they're given much more responsibility and higher status.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。现在很多毕业生从事这类工作……我原以为19世纪农业工人的数量会有更大的变化。但1871年的人口普查显示，大约25%的人口在土地上工作。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: A lot of graduates go in for this kind of work now ...

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: I'd expected there to be a much bigger change in the number of agricultural workers in the 19th century.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: But the 1871 census showed that roughly 25% of the population worked on the land.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: Yeah, I'd have assumed it would be more than 50%.
segments.zh: 是啊，我本以为会超过50%。现在不到0.2%。

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: Now it's less than 0.2%.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: What about care workers?
segments.zh: 那护理人员呢？

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: They barely existed in the 19th century as people's lifespan was so much shorter.
segments.zh: 他们在19世纪几乎不存在，因为人们的寿命短得多。但现在这个行业当然会看到巨大的增长。

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: But now of course this sector will see huge growth.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: Yeah – and it's hard enough to meet current demand.
segments.zh: 是啊——现在满足现有需求都够难的了。银行柜员的未来看起来相当暗淡。自从八十年代引入自动取款机以来，这个岗位就在减少。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: The future looks quite bleak for bank clerks.

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: They've been in decline since ATMs were introduced in the eighties.

segments.speaker: YOUNG MAN
segments.en: And technology will certainly make most of the jobs they do now redundant, I think.
segments.zh: 而且我认为技术肯定会让他们现在做的大部分工作变得多余。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: I agree, although the situation may change.
segments.zh: 我同意，尽管情况可能会改变。很难预测会发生什么。

segments.speaker: YOUNG WOMAN
segments.en: It's very hard to predict what will happen.

items.prompt: Which TWO opinions about the Luddites do the students express?

pairs.note: 题干问对Luddites的看法，原文用'felt so threatened'表达他们的感受，是观点同义替换。
题干未直接出现，但原文否定'positive thing'暗示负面观点，帮助定位。

paraphrase.explain: 通过'felt so threatened'和'didn't seem a positive thing'定位到学生表达Luddites感到威胁、认为技术不积极的观点。

items.prompt: Which TWO opinions about the Luddites do the students express?

pairs.note: 题干问对Luddites的看法，原文用'felt so threatened'表达他们的感受，是观点替换。
题干说'students express'，原文'I can see why'是学生表达观点的标志。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句'I can see why the Luddites felt so threatened'中，'I can see why'对应题干'students express'，'felt so threatened'对应'opinions about the Luddites'，由此定位到学生表达的观点。

items.prompt: Which TWO predictions about the future of work are the students doubtful about?

pairs.note: doubtful about 和 not so sure about 都表示怀疑，是同义替换。
题干中的 lower unemployment 在原文中完全一样，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 学生说 'I'm not so sure about that' 中的 'that' 指代前一句的 'lower unemployment'，所以定位到学生对 lower unemployment 持怀疑态度。

items.prompt: Which TWO predictions about the future of work are the students doubtful about?

pairs.note: doubtful about 与 not so sure about 同义，表示怀疑。
题干中的预测内容，原文直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 学生说 'I'm not so sure about that'，其中 'that' 指代前文提到的 'lower unemployment'，因此定位到学生对 'lower unemployment' 这一预测表示怀疑。

items.prompt: What comment do the students make about each of the following jobs?

pairs.note: 题干comment对应原文incredible，表示评价。
题干中的accountants在原文中直接出现。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的accountants定位到原文中提及accountants的句子，并找到表示评价的incredible。

items.prompt: What comment do the students make about each of the following jobs?

pairs.note: 原文直接出现，与题干中的'jobs'对应，指代个人服务类工作。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是关于某些工作的评论，定位句提到'personal services'，与题干中的'jobs'对应，因此答案在此。

items.prompt: What comment do the students make about each of the following jobs?

pairs.p: Really boring compared to these days
pairs.note: 题干问'评论'，原文用'Really boring'表达评价，是观点类同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干关键词'comment'定位到原文中表达观点的句子，其中'Really boring'是对工作的评论。

items.prompt: What comment do the students make about each of the following jobs?

pairs.note: comment 被替换为 assumed，表示学生表达的看法

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的 comment 定位到原文中的 assumed，学生在此处发表评论。

items.prompt: What comment do the students make about each of the following jobs?

pairs.note: 题干中的'barely existed'与原文完全一致，直接定位。
时间信息直接对应，帮助锁定位置。
题干中的'growth'与原文'huge growth'对应，但注意原文是'will see huge growth'，表示未来增长。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的'barely existed'和'19th century'直接定位到原文第一句，再根据'growth'找到对应答案。

items.prompt: What comment do the students make about each of the following jobs?

pairs.note: 题干问'comment'（评论），原文用'think'（认为）表达类似观点，属于同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句中的'think'对应题干的'comment'，表明这是学生对工作的评论。
